patterns_recognized: Home-favorite overconfidence (model_prob > 0.65): two high-confidence home-favorite losses on 2026-05-08 (SEA@CHW 74%, NYM@ARI 68%) confirm systematic over-rating; default DOWNGRADE when model exceeds 65% on home teams.

Negative-edge GOLD picks lose reliably: CHC@TEX (-4.41pp) and NYY@MIL (-2.00pp) on 2026-05-09 both lost; edge must be > 0pp to sustain GOLD direction — market holds SP-day form and lineup info the model doesn't see.

Rookie/small-sample SP blindspot: Misiorowski blowout (2026-05-08) and repeated asterisked small-sample flags show model mis-prices matchups when one SP has < 30 IP current season; Trevor McDonald (SF @ LAD) triggers this flag today.
Stacked pipeline flags (confidence_downgrade=True + acute_roster=True or fair_prob < 0.42) = reliable automatic DOWNGRADE; validated 2-for-2 on 2026-05-09 (COL@PHI, DET@KC).

LAA @ CLE.reasoning: CLE SP is Joey Cantillo (confirmed, id=676282); LAA SP is still unannounced at model-scoring time. CLE at 21-21 hosts LAA at 16-25 — meaningful quality gap favoring the home side. Cantillo is a functional MLB starter vs an unknown LAA arm. Model correctly withheld a pick pending LAA SP confirmation. Once LAA SP resolves, expect CLE as a moderate SKIP-tier favorite. No historical postgame pattern flags a concern beyond the missing data itself.

NYY @ BAL.reasoning: NYY (26-15) sends Ryan Weathers vs BAL's Brandon Young (id=687064, high player ID indicating a 2025/2026 debut, likely fewer than 10 MLB starts). Rookie SP blindspot applies: if Young's thin Statcast sample drives BAL's SP-edge feature, that confidence is unreliable. NYY road quality is substantial. Expect NYY as the correct directional pick once SP data scores; watch that model does not inflate BAL's edge on Young's small-sample data.

TB @ TOR.reasoning: TB (26-13, AL East leader) sends Drew Rasmussen vs Kevin Gausman — a genuine ace who meaningfully levels this matchup. A large Stage 1/2 gap is likely (Gausman dominates F5, TB bullpen closes). Per 2026-05-09 PIT@SF lesson, do NOT auto-downgrade a large Stage 1/2 gap when the picked team has strong F2_xwoba offensive signal; TB's lineup quality is real. Coin-flip result expected; SKIP until full model data is available.

ARI @ TEX.reasoning: ARI (19-20) sends Michael Soroka vs TEX's Nathan Eovaldi — two experienced veterans with serviceable profiles. Both teams near .500; TEX earns a slim home-field edge. Soroka's injury-comeback history limits large SP-feature sample accumulation; Eovaldi is durable. Market will be close; model edge expected to be thin. No postgame pattern flags this matchup. SKIP tier is appropriate; CONFIRM the model's PENDING stance.

SEA @ HOU.reasoning: George Kirby (SEA, id=669923) is one of the AL's elite SPs; Peter Lambert (HOU) is moderate. HOU sits 16-25, the worst record among today's six home teams. The SP quality gap is large and supports a genuine SEA road edge. Home-favorite overconfidence heuristic does not apply here since HOU is a home underdog, not a home favorite. Warning: if model somehow produces HOU at >60% despite HOU's poor record and the Lambert vs Kirby mismatch, DOWNGRADE immediately.

SF @ LAD.reasoning: Trevor McDonald (SF, id=686790) is almost certainly a 2025/2026 MLB debut with fewer than 10 starts — rookie SP blindspot applies directly. LAD (24-16) hosting SF (16-24) with Roki Sasaki is a genuine quality edge, but two heuristics force DOWNGRADE: (1) home-favorite overconfidence — LAD will likely score above 65% model prob, a known failure zone; (2) McDonald's thin Statcast sample inflates LAD's SP-edge feature beyond reliable calibration. Pre-emptively downgrading from whatever tier the model assigns to SKIP pending SP sample verification.
